human_note_source: HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 Item 4 (substance via EXP-257; this rework only addresses mechanical fixes)
rework_summary: EXP-257 substance was approved by curmudgeon c1 (qid 268) — questions verbatim, link targets resolve, placement story sound, defense_survives 0. Three mechanical fixes required before integration: (1) re-anchor segment EP2 to .ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption (the .verdict-bars -> ds-verdict-bars prefix migration in ISS-1571/1572/1573 landed AFTER EXP-257 was drafted, invalidating the original anchor literal); (2) rename all 9 new CSS classes to ds- prefix in BOTH segment EP1 (HTML class= attributes) and segment EP2 (CSS selectors) per ISS-1573 invariant; (3) switch color:var(--ink-3) to color:var(--ink-2) on three text-color uses (.ds-ep-heading, .ds-ep-num, .ds-ep-where) to clear the dark-mode WCAG AA gap that ISS-1580 already flagged on EXP-256. Two minor fixes also folded in: ASCII-apostrophe (model's not model’s) in question 2 to match eg-evaluate body copy; caption text 'Read the long form ->' instead of 'Full preface ->' to set correct expectation that the destination is a collapsed <details> block. Substance unchanged — same 5 questions, same link targets, same placement, same kernel-of-truth.
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — same insertion points as EXP-257 (one HTML segment immediately before the existing <section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">; one CSS segment immediately after the existing .ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...} rule). Substance is verbatim from EXP-257; only the 5 mechanical/typographic differences listed in rework_summary apply.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique on workspace AND raw GitHub main as of this run — `grep -c` returns exactly 1 in build-scripts/generate-html.js (149,980 bytes, identical to GitHub raw). Anchor is stable post-EXP-255/256 integration plans; the ds- prefix migration that broke EP2 did not touch this anchor (accuracy-math-section retained its name).
patch_segments.pre_apply_grep_assertion: grep -c '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST equal 1; abort apply if 0 or >1
patch_segments.insert_text:
Five questions to ask of any scientific claim

From this review or any other — each question links to where this review tests it. Read the long form →

  1. 1 Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives? Standard Model Explains (3.4)
  2. 2 Can the prediction be derived from the model's own parameters? Self-Contradicted (3.3)
  3. 3 Is the cited data accurately represented? Refuted by Data (3.2)
  4. 4 Is the claim falsifiable? Misleading & Unfalsifiable (3.6)
  5. 5 Are the same data being counted multiple times? De-Duplication Analysis (6.6b)
mechanical_changes_vs_EXP_257: All 9 class= attributes now ds- prefixed: ds-evaluate-preface, ds-ep-heading, ds-ep-caption, ds-ep-jump, ds-ep-rows, ds-ep-row, ds-ep-num, ds-ep-q, ds-ep-where. id attributes (id="ep-heading") and aria-labelledby targets are unchanged because the ISS-1573 invariant scope is CSS class names; ids are not part of the design-system naming convention.
Question 2 apostrophe: 'model’s' -> 'model\'s' (ASCII apostrophe) to match the existing eg-evaluate body copy at docs/index.html:481/502 verbatim (those use the straight ASCII apostrophe).

Caption text: 'Full preface ->' -> 'Read the long form ->' to set correct expectation that the destination is a collapsed <details id="eg-evaluate"> block — readers click the caption, land on tab 'evaluate', see the section anchor, and may need to click <summary> to expand. The new wording 'long form' implies depth that may need to be expanded; 'Full preface' implied the long form was one click away.
patch_segments.collision_note: No collision with EXP-255 (sc-hero rework, lines ~1366-1383) or EXP-256 (falsifiability-module insert, after generateVerdictBarChart() call). Visual flow after all three integrate: title-block -> verdict-bar -> falsifiability-module (EXP-256) -> thesis-hero (EXP-255) -> ds-evaluate-preface (EXP-264) -> accuracy-math-section.
patch_segments.author_intent_note: Per curmudgeon c1 stronger_argument #2: if the EXP-255/256/264 trio is integrated together, sequence them and re-run anchor greps between commits. Concurrent application of multi-anchor patches against a moving target is exactly how the EXP-257 prefix-mismatch slipped past anchor_uniqueness.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique on workspace AND raw GitHub main as of this run — `grep -c` returns exactly 1; the OLD anchor literal `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` (used by EXP-257 verbatim) returns 0 because of the ISS-1571/1572/1573 ds- prefix migration. EXP-264 uses the post-migration literal.
patch_segments.pre_apply_grep_assertion: grep -c '\.ds-verdict-bars \.ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST equal 1; abort apply if 0 or >1
patch_segments.anchor_repair_diff_vs_EXP_257: EXP-257 anchor: '.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}'. EXP-264 anchor: '.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}'. Two literal substring substitutions: '.verdict-bars'->'.ds-verdict-bars', '.vb-caption'->'.ds-vb-caption'. Same rule body, same uniqueness, same insertion semantics.
mechanical_changes_vs_EXP_257: All 9 class selectors prefixed: .evaluate-preface->.ds-evaluate-preface and the 8 .ep-* selectors->.ds-ep-* (compound selectors like '.evaluate-preface .ep-heading' become '.ds-evaluate-preface .ds-ep-heading'). 13 distinct CSS rules updated; no semantics change.

Three ink-3 -> ink-2 swaps for WCAG AA dark-mode fix (.ds-ep-heading color, .ds-ep-num color, .ds-ep-where color). Curmudgeon c1 measured: dark mode --ink-3 (#8a8680) on --card-bg (#222240) = 4.23:1, FAILS AA 4.5:1; --ink-2 (#b8b5ad) = 7.48:1, PASSES. Light mode unaffected (5.36:1 -> 7.69:1, both pass). Verified independently: rgb(184,181,173) vs rgb(34,34,64) yields contrast ratio 7.48 to two decimals.

Border-left:3px var(--ink-3) intentionally NOT changed — border colors are non-textual and not subject to WCAG AA text contrast. Visual differentiation from EXP-256's --accent left rail is preserved (the methodology-vs-verdict-criteria signal).
patch_segments.design_token_audit: All colors routed through CSS variables. Border-left uses --ink-3 (deliberately distinct from the EXP-256 falsifiability-module's --accent left rail). ds-ep-heading / ds-ep-num / ds-ep-where text now use --ink-2 (was --ink-3). ds-ep-caption uses --ink-2 (was already --ink-2 in EXP-257; unchanged). ds-ep-q uses --heading with --accent on hover. Background --card-bg matches existing legend cards.
patch_segments.collision_note: No collision. Anchor sits one rule above where EXP-255 inserted (EXP-255 inserts between .ds-sc-domains and .ds-verdict-bars{margin:...}); EXP-256 inserts after the .accuracy-math-section @media rule (several lines above this anchor). Both are unaffected by EXP-264.

grep_self_test.purpose: Per curmudgeon c1 stronger_argument #1: pre-apply assert anchor uniqueness, post-apply verify the patch landed.
pre_apply_assertions: grep -c '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST equal 1
grep -c '\.ds-evaluate-preface\b' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST equal 0 (idempotency: don't double-apply)
post_apply_assertions: grep -cE '\.evaluate-preface\b|\.ep-heading\b|\.ep-caption\b|\.ep-jump\b|\.ep-rows\b|\.ep-row\b|\.ep-num\b|\.ep-q\b|\.ep-where\b' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # MUST equal 0 (no unprefixed leak)

cross_references_unchanged_from_EXP_257: All five link targets (#stdmodel, #selfcon, #refuted, #misleading, #dedup-analysis) and the caption target (#eg-evaluate) carry over verbatim from EXP-257. Per-question rationale unchanged (curmudgeon c1 endorsed the routing including the question-4 -> #misleading judgment call).

light_mode.note: both pass AA; switching to ink-2 raises margin

dark_mode.note: ink-3 FAILED AA (<4.5:1); ink-2 passes with margin. Verified by independent re-derivation (sRGB -> linear -> relative luminance, contrast formula (L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05)).

wcag_audit_after_fix.border_color_left_alone_rationale: border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3) is a 3px non-textual visual element. WCAG 1.4.11 (non-text contrast) applies a 3:1 threshold to UI components, which both light (#6E675B vs #fafafa) and dark (#8a8680 vs #222240) modes meet.

kernel_of_truth: EXP-257 substance was sound — promoting eg-evaluate's 5-question methodology to a scannable Overview preface gives readers a portable evaluation framework with one-click drill-downs to the verdict buckets that demonstrate each failure mode. The mechanical fixes are not about substance; they are about honoring two design-system invariants (ds- prefix on new classes per ISS-1573, dark-mode WCAG AA on text colors per ISS-1580 precedent) and one staleness gap (EP2 anchor invalidated by a between-draft-and-queue-push prefix migration).
why_the_kernel_doesnt_save_the_patch_as_: Even though the substance is approved, applying EXP-257 verbatim would: (a) silently no-op the CSS insert (the .verdict-bars .vb-caption literal does not exist in current main, so the entire CSS block does not get inserted); (b) leak 9 unprefixed CSS classes that ISS-1573 closed as invariant violations; (c) reproduce ISS-1580's open dark-mode WCAG gap on three text declarations. The substance survives the patch as-rewritten in EXP-264; the rework is purely mechanical.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just patch EXP-257 in place rather than create a new EXP-264?
anticipated_objections.response: Two reasons. (1) The decider tracking model expects new EXP entries for rework cycles (EXP-261 was the rework of EXP-255 under the same pattern; EXP-262 was an EXP-254 follow-on). Editing EXP-257 in place would lose the audit trail of what the curmudgeon flagged and what was fixed. (2) The expansion-tracker.json EXP-264 entry was created by the decider/curmudgeon flow specifically to point at this rework — the tracker entry is the contract.

anticipated_objections.objection: ISS-1571/1572/1573 closed the EXP-255 prefix gap. Why not also re-baseline EXP-256 (which c1 noted shipped without prefix on .fm-* classes)?
anticipated_objections.response: ISS-1580 is open and tracks exactly that. EXP-256 falsifiability-module rework would be a separate EXP, parallel to this one. EXP-264's scope is strictly EXP-257 mechanical fixes; co-mingling EXP-256 cleanup would expand scope and risk conflating two reworks. Recommend the decider open a parallel EXP for EXP-256 if/when ISS-1580 is reopened with rework intent.

anticipated_objections.objection: The ASCII-apostrophe fix in question 2 is cosmetic. Why is it folded into a 'mechanical fixes' rework?
anticipated_objections.response: Curmudgeon c1 explicitly recommended it: 'Either ASCII-apostrophe both surfaces or curly both — pick one. Lowest effort: ASCII in the patch, matching the eg-evaluate body copy.' Folding it in costs zero scope; not folding it leaves a known cosmetic inconsistency that a future curmudgeon pass will flag again.

anticipated_objections.objection: The caption rephrasing ('Read the long form ->' vs 'Full preface ->') is also flagged 'Optional' by c1. Why include it?
anticipated_objections.response: Same logic as Q2 — zero-scope-cost typography fix that closes a curmudgeon-flagged minor and prevents re-discovery. The new wording also more honestly describes the destination (long-form prose, possibly collapsed) without claiming a shorter trip ('one click') than the user actually gets.

anticipated_objections.objection: If a future ds- migration sweeps another existing rule, won't EP2's new anchor go stale again?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — but the patch ships with explicit pre-apply grep assertions (in patch_segments[1].grep_self_test) that the decider should run as gating checks. If anchor uniqueness fails, the apply aborts with a loud error; the analyst then re-anchors and re-queues. This converts a silent-no-op failure mode (the EXP-257 case) into a loud-fail mode. Per c1 stronger_argument #1, this is the right invariant to add to all patch_segments-style proposals going forward.

anticipated_objections.objection: id values (id="ep-heading") are not ds-prefixed. Doesn't the design system also cover ids?
anticipated_objections.response: ISS-1573's invariant text reads 'EXP-255 new CSS classes missing ds- prefix' — scope is CSS class names. id values are out of scope: the codebase has dozens of unprefixed ids (id="eg-evaluate", id="stdmodel", id="selfcon", id="misleading", etc.) that were not touched by the ds- migration. Touching id values here would expand scope and break dozens of inbound href fragments. EXP-264 follows the existing precedent.

other_side_of_the_argument: A defender of EXP-257-as-written could argue that c1's three findings are over-strict: the ds- prefix invariant is recent, EXP-256 shipped without it and only got follow-up issues, and the dark-mode contrast gap is below threshold by 0.27 (4.23 vs 4.5). The counter is that these are closed precedents (ISS-1573 explicitly closed the EXP-255 prefix gap with 'fixed' status) and one open precedent (ISS-1580). Letting EXP-264 ship with the same gaps would compound the technical debt that ISS-1580 is already tracking. The mechanical fixes are cheap (zero substance change) and align EXP-264 with the design-system contract that the codebase is converging toward.
holes_addressed: Curmudgeon c1 critical finding (segment EP2 broken anchor): re-anchored to .ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption (verified unique on workspace and raw GitHub main).

Curmudgeon c1 moderate finding (9 new CSS classes missing ds- prefix per ISS-1573): all 9 classes now ds- prefixed in both segment EP1 (HTML class= attributes) and segment EP2 (CSS selectors, including compound selectors like .ds-evaluate-preface .ds-ep-heading).

Curmudgeon c1 moderate finding (dark-mode WCAG AA gap on three ink-3 text declarations): switched .ds-ep-heading, .ds-ep-num, .ds-ep-where to color:var(--ink-2) (light mode 7.69:1, dark mode 7.48:1, both pass AA with margin).
Curmudgeon c1 minor finding (Q2 typography divergence model’s vs model's): switched to ASCII apostrophe to match eg-evaluate body copy verbatim.
Curmudgeon c1 minor finding (caption verbiage implies one-click access to long-form): rephrased to 'Read the long form ->' to honestly signal the destination is a collapsed <details> block.

Curmudgeon c1 stronger_argument #1 (add grep self-test to patch package): segment EP1 and EP2 both ship with explicit pre_apply_grep_assertion fields and EP2 ships with a grep_self_test object documenting pre- and post-apply checks for the decider to run as gating.
curmudgeon_review_focus_for_c2: Verify segment EP2's new anchor literal `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}` is unique in current main at the time of c2 review (re-grep — anchors can stale between drafts).
Verify the 9 new CSS class names land with ds- prefix in both segments (no unprefixed leaks).
Verify the three ink-2 swaps are present in the CSS insert and the contrast claims (light 7.69:1, dark 7.48:1) reproduce on independent calculation.
Verify Q2 ASCII-apostrophe is present in the HTML insert.
Verify caption text reads 'Read the long form →' not 'Full preface →'.
Apply c1 stronger_argument #2: confirm sequencing recommendation (one EXP at a time, re-grep between commits) is captured in decider_instructions.
Confirm border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3) is intentionally retained (non-textual element, WCAG 1.4.11 UI-components 3:1 threshold met).
acceptance_criteria: After build: docs/index.html contains exactly one `<section class="ds-evaluate-preface">` block.
After build: docs/index.html contains zero unprefixed `evaluate-preface`, `ep-heading`, `ep-caption`, `ep-jump`, `ep-rows`, `ep-row`, `ep-num`, `ep-q`, `ep-where` selectors or class= attributes.
After build: all five question links resolve (#stdmodel, #selfcon, #refuted, #misleading, #dedup-analysis present as id attributes in docs/index.html).
After build: caption link `#eg-evaluate` resolves to the existing id and reads 'Read the long form ->' (not 'Full preface ->').
After build: question 2 contains the ASCII apostrophe in 'model's own parameters' (no ’ entity in the question text).

After build: CSS rules `.ds-evaluate-preface .ds-ep-heading{...color:var(--ink-2)...}`, `.ds-evaluate-preface .ds-ep-num{...color:var(--ink-2)...}`, `.ds-evaluate-preface .ds-ep-where{...color:var(--ink-2)...}` are all present.
After build: CSS rule `.ds-evaluate-preface{...border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3)...}` is present (border color intentionally retained).
Existing tests pass (node test.js — count to be queried at integration time, not hardcoded here).
Above-the-fold check on 1440x900: thesis-hero (EXP-255) remains visible without scrolling; ds-evaluate-preface starts visible or just at the fold.
Below-720px responsive: ds-ep-row collapses to 2-column grid with .ds-ep-where below the question (grid-template-areas swap).
out_of_scope_explicit: Does NOT modify data/sections.json — no prose change required.
Does NOT modify the eg-evaluate section itself.
Does NOT depend on EXP-190 (Scoreboard, parked).
Does NOT touch the EXP-255 thesis-hero or EXP-256 falsifiability-module patches — orthogonal changes.
Does NOT add any new template variables or build-pipeline dependencies — pure static HTML/CSS additive insert.
Does NOT re-baseline EXP-256 falsifiability-module to ds- prefix (separate scope tracked under ISS-1580).
Does NOT modify showTab() to auto-expand collapsed <details> on arrival — c1 noted this as an alternative-to-caption-rephrasing fix; rephrasing was chosen as the lower-effort option.
decider_instructions: Apply both patch_segments to build-scripts/generate-html.js. PRE-APPLY: run the grep assertions in each segment's pre_apply_grep_assertion field — if any assertion fails, abort the apply and re-queue for the analyst. POST-APPLY: run the post_apply_assertions in patch_segments[1].grep_self_test — verify the patch landed and no unprefixed selectors leaked. Then `node build.js html` (regenerate docs/index.html), then `node test.js` (regression check). Per c1 stronger_argument #2: if the EXP-255/256/264 trio is integrated together, sequence them and re-run anchor greps between commits. Suggested curmudgeon routing for c2: target_type='expansion-rework', verify_focus='mechanical-only' — substance unchanged from EXP-257 c1.

build_impact.new_lines_net: approximately +30 net (HTML insert ~25 lines + CSS insert ~14 lines, no removals)
build_impact.test_impact: no test changes expected; existing tests should continue to pass

notes_for_decider: EXP-264 is the rework of EXP-257 per curmudgeon c1 (qid 268). Same-pattern as EXP-261 reworked EXP-255 after curmudgeon c1 of EXP-255 (ISS-1571/1572/1573). After EXP-264 integrates and curmudgeon c2 spot-checks, HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 Item 4 is delivered (the trio EXP-255/256/264 covers all four HNOTE items). Strongly recommend NOT applying the EXP-257 patch text any longer — supersede it with EXP-264 for the same insertion points. Optional: at integration time, also fold the c1 stronger_argument #1 invariant (pre-apply grep self-test) into the decider's standard-procedure for any patch_segments-style proposal — this would have caught the EXP-257 anchor staleness automatically.
tldr_evidence: Curmudgeon c1 (qid 268) approved the substance of EXP-257 (verdict change not warranted, defense_survives 0) but flagged three mechanical defects: a stale CSS anchor invalidated by the ds- prefix migration after EXP-257 was drafted, nine new CSS classes missing the ds- design-system prefix invariant from ISS-1573, and three text-color declarations failing dark-mode WCAG AA contrast at 4.23:1.
tldr_verdict: EXP-264 reapplies EXP-257's substance (same 5 questions, same link targets, same placement) with the three mechanical fixes baked in: re-anchor segment EP2 to .ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption, ds- prefix on all 9 new CSS classes in both HTML and CSS segments, color:var(--ink-2) in place of var(--ink-3) on .ds-ep-heading / .ds-ep-num / .ds-ep-where (clears dark-mode AA at 7.48:1). Two minor c1 fixes also folded in: ASCII apostrophe in question 2 and caption text 'Read the long form ->' in place of 'Full preface ->'.
authored_by: analyst (scheduled, 2026-04-27T02:09:13.559Z, Mode 1 EXP-264 rework of EXP-257)
